title: update OCI image-spec to 775207bd45b6cb8153ce218cc59351799217451f
body:
This mainly pulls in the latest support for zstd-compressed layers and
eases testing of containers/image.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body: @mtrmac, PTAL. This is really just a way to simplify testing https://github.com/containers/image/pull/563 - it avoids pushing first to c/image and then revendoring here.
The changes LGTM, but I'm not understanding how they ease testing?

> The changes LGTM, but I'm not understanding how they ease testing?

The CI in containers/image `git clone`s Skopeo master to run the PR against the Skopeo tests. But it only "injects" containers/image in `./vendor`. If the PR of containers/image requires to change other dependencies, then the CI run fails because we cannot compile Skopeo.

That's the case in https://github.com/containers/image/pull/563. Adding the dependency here will make https://github.com/containers/image/pull/563 compile again. Usually we have a separate PR open at Skopeo to let the c/image PR run through the CI but in this case I figured we can just do the vendor PR in advance - it'll spare some cycles/energy as I don't have to push twice but only once.
